name: FOG Design+Art, San Francisco
text: Photo Illustration: Kat BrownIn 2026, there is no shortage of art fairs taking place around the world. When it comes to the art market this year, all eyes will be on the Gulf region as Art Basel launches a fair in Qatar this February and Frieze takes over Abu Dhabi Art, which runs in November. One other major change to the calendar is that Frieze Seoul and the Armory Show will no longer conflict w

name: ART SG, Singapore
text: ART SG, SingaporeImage Credit: Courtesy Marina Bay SandsStaged at the iconic Marina Bay Sands, ART SG will return for its fourth edition, bringing together95 exhibitors. The 2026 iteration will introduce a performance art section, via a new partnership with Shanghai’s Rockbund Art Museum, whose executive director and chief curator, X-Zhu Nowell, will take on the role of the fair’s film and perform

name: Art Basel Qatar
text: Art Basel QatarImage Credit: Julius Hirtzberger/Courtesy Art BaselIn May 2025, Art Basel announced that it would launch a brand-new fair in Doha, the capital city of Qatar. Produced in partnership with Qatar Sports Investments (QSI) and QC+, Art Basel Qatar will bring together 87 exhibitors from 31 countries and territories for its inaugural edition, which will be split across multiple venues in d

name: Mexico City Art Week
text: Mexico City Art WeekImage Credit: Courtesy Zona MacoMexico City will once again light up in February with four fairs: Zona Maco, Feria Material, Salón Acme, and Unique Design X.Zona Macois one of the most important fairs in Latin America and includes sections dedicated to design, historical objects, photography, and books alongside its main contemporary art section. The 2026 edition of the fair wi

name: 1-54 Marrakech
text: 1-54 MarrakechImage Credit: ©Nicholas Brasseur, Say WhoA sharp look at contemporary art scenes across Africa and its diaspora, 1-54 Marrakech is among the smallest of the world’s art fairs but with an outsize impact, featuring top galleries from around the world in conversation with Moroccan enterprises. The 2026 edition will bring together around 20 exhibitors.Location: La Mamounia HotelPreview D

name: India Art Fair, New Delhi
text: India Art Fair, New DelhiImage Credit: Courtesy India Art FairThe India Art Fair is the subcontinent’s largest art fair, gathering around130 exhibitorsfor its 2026 edition. This year’s iteration will see the introduction of Swali Craft Prize; an expanded performance art program, organized by Nikhil Chopra and HH Art Spaces; and a reimagining of its facade, which will be done by Afrah Shafiq, as pa

name: Frieze Los Angeles
text: Frieze Los AngelesImage Credit: Casey Kelbaugh/Courtesy Frieze and CKAThe first of Frieze’s international fairs to take place each year, Frieze LA will bring together95 exhibitorsfrom 22 countries to the Santa Monica Airport. The fair’s Focus section will again be organized by independent curator Essence Harden, while the Art Production Fund will return to organize the public program. NXTHVN will

name: Outsider Art Fair, New York
text: Outsider Art Fair, New YorkImage Credit: Olya VysotskayaFor its 2026 edition, the Outsider Art Fair in New York will bring together around 70 exhibitors and will be the firstunder the directionof former dealer Elizabeth Denny.Location: Metropolitan PavilionPreview Day: Thursday, March 19General Admission Days: Friday, March 20–Sunday, March 22Website.ReadARTnews’s past coverage of the Outsider Art

name: ARCOmadrid
text: ARCOmadridImage Credit: Courtesy ARCO MadridCelebrating its 45th edition in 2026, ARCOmadrid will bring together 170 galleries in its main section. It will also feature three curated sections: “ARCO2045: El Futuro, Por Ahora,” curated by José Luis Blondet (of LACMA) and Magali Arriola (formerly of Museo Tamayo); “Opening. New galleries,” organized by independent curators Rafa Barber and Anissa Tou

name: TEFAF Maastricht
text: TEFAF MaastrichtImage Credit: Courtesy TEFAFTEFAF Maastricht will bring together276 exhibitorsfrom 24 countries for its 2026 edition, who will present art and antiquities spanning 7,000 years. Among the first-time exhibitors are GRIMM, Ceysson & Bénétière, and Proyectos Monclova Gallery. In addition to its main section, TEFAF Maastrict also includes “Focus,” for single-artist presentations of pion

name: Art Basel Hong Kong
text: Art Basel Hong KongImage Credit: Courtesy Art BaselFor its2026 edition, Art Basel Hong Kong bring together 240 exhibitors, with more than half of the them operating a space in the Asia-Pacific region, including 29 with locations in Hong Kong. This edition will also see 32 galleries participating for the first time. The upcoming edition will introduce a new section to the fair, called Echoes, for w

name: SP-Arte, São Paulo
text: SP-Arte, São PauloImage Credit: Courtesy SP-ArteSP-Arte, Brazil’s largest art fair, will host its 22nd edition in 2026. With around 180 exhibitors, the fair brings together some of Latin America’s most important and closely watched galleries together alongside a selection of international exhibitors. The 2026 edition will relaunch the Showcase sector for international exhibitors showing at the fai

name: IFPDA Print Fair
text: IFPDA Print FairImage Credit: Annie ForrestIn November 2025, the International Fine Print Dealers Associationannouncedthat it would become the International Fine Prints & Drawings Association. This name change is meant to signify the association’s embrace of dealers of drawings to its membership and its annual IFPDA Print Fair. The 2026 edition will be the first since this change, the first in 30

name: Expo Chicago
text: Expo ChicagoImage Credit: Courtesy Choose ChicagoPart of the Frieze portfoliosince 2023, Expo Chicago’s 2026 edition will be its 13th and the firstunder the directionof Kate Sierzputowski, with Essence Harden serving as curator. While its exhibitor list has still not been announced, the forthcoming edition will be “a more focused fair with a smaller floorplan, designed to highlight the strongest w

name: Dallas Art Fair and Dallas Invitational
text: Dallas Art Fair and Dallas InvitationalImage Credit: Daniel DrienskyLong a bastion of collecting, Dallas now boasts two art fairs that run concurrently each spring. The long-running Dallas Art Fair will host its 18th edition in 2026. The younger Dallas Invitational will mount its fourth edition this year; operating under an invite-only model, the fair plans to increase its footprint for 2026. Both

name: AIPAD Photography Show
text: AIPAD Photography ShowImage Credit: Photo Erica PriceEach year, the Association of International Photography Art Dealers (AIPAD) gathers together around 75 exhibitors, primarily drawn from its membership, for the Photography Show, one of a number of medium-focused art fairs. With the2026 edition, the fair will introduce a new section, Focal Point, for solo presentations of lens-based artists.Locat

name: Art Brussels
text: Art BrusselsImage Credit: JJ DE NEYERFor its 2025 edition, Art Brussels will bring together some 135 galleries to two halls within the iconic Brussels Expo, an Art Deco structure built in 1935.Location: Brussels ExpoPreview Day: Thursday, April 23General Admission Days: Friday, April 24–Sunday, April 26Website.

name: New York Art Week (May)
text: New York Art Week (May)Image Credit: Casey KelbaughThe 2026 editions of five New York art fairs will coincide over the course of one week this year: Frieze New York, NADA New York, Independent, TEFAF New York, and 1-54 New York. While the fairs have yet to announce their exhibitor lists, they will once again bring together hundreds of dealers from around the country and the world at various venues

name: Art Dubai
text: Art DubaiImage Credit: Courtesy Electra, Official Contractor of Art DubaiWhile Art Dubai might maintain its status as the largest art fair in the Middle East this year, it will face serious competition from Art Basel Qatar (in February) and Frieze Abu Dhabi (in November). Marking its 20th anniversary in 2026, this year’s edition, the first under the direction of Dunja Gottweis, will bring together

name: Art Basel, Switzerland
text: Art Basel, SwitzerlandImage Credit: ©Art BaselArt Basel’s Swiss edition is often considered the world’s world’s top art fair, for which dealers typically save their best—and priciest—works. While its exhibitor list won’t be announced for another few months, the fair typically gathers together some 250 galleries. So far, Basel has announced that Ruba Katrib, chief curator of MoMA PS1, will organize

name: Frieze Seoul and Kiaf
text: Frieze Seoul and KiafImage Credit: Jung Yeon-Je/AFP via Getty ImagesFor its fifth edition, Frieze Seoul will return to the massive COEX convention center in the Korean capital. Frieze Seoul typically draws top international blue-chip galleries to Seoul, many of which have since established outposts in the city; the 2025 edition featured 116 exhibitors. For 2026, its Focus section will expand its p

name: Sydney Contemporary
text: Sydney ContemporaryImage Credit: James Horan Photography Pty LTD 2022For its 2026 edition, Sydney Contemporary, Australia’s largest art fair, will bring together more than 100 galleries, with a strong showing of exhibitors from Australia and across the Asia-Pacific region.Location: CarriageworksPreview Day: Wednesday, September 2General Admission Days: Thursday, September 3–Sunday, September 6Webs

name: Tokyo Gendai, Yokohama
text: Tokyo Gendai, YokohamaImage Credit: Courtesy Tokyo GendaiAfter hosting its first two editions in early July, Tokyo Gendaishifted its dateslast year to the second week of September, which it will keep for 2026. Taking place the week after Frieze Seoul, these dates pose the same dilemma for visitors as Frieze London and Art Basel Paris currently do, since those two fairs are also held in back-to-bac

name: The Armory Show and Independent 20th Century
text: The Armory Show and Independent 20th CenturyImage Credit: Maximilíano Durón/ARTnewsSince joining Frieze’s portfolio in 2023, the Armory Show has conflicted with Frieze Seoul, both running the first week of September. For 2026, that will no longer be the case, as the fair shifts to later in September, away from its post–Labor Day, back-to-school vibes it has had since it changed venues to the Javit

name: Atlanta Art Fair
text: Atlanta Art FairImage Credit: Courtesy AMPNow in its third edition, the Atlanta Art Fair will return to Pullman Yards for 2026. The fair’s exhibitor list will be released later this year, with curator Nato Thompson continuing his role as artistic director. (The fair is part of the portfolio of AMP, which runs three other fairs throughout the year: San Francisco Art Fair, Seattle Art Fair, Art on P

name: Untitled Art, Houston
text: Untitled Art, HoustonImage Credit: Courtesy Houston FirstAfter having expanded to Houston in 2025, Untitled Art, Houston will stage its sophomore edition this year. While the inaugural edition, which hosted 88 exhibitors, took place in mid-September, the upcoming fair will shift to the first week of October.Location: George R. Brown Convention CenterPreview Day: Thursday, October 1General Admissio

name: Frieze London and Frieze Masters
text: Frieze London and Frieze MastersImage Credit: Maximilíano Durón/ARTnewsFrieze London, the fair company’s hallmark contemporary art event in the British capital, runs each October in a custom-built tent in the Regent’s Park. Just across the park, in another custom tent, is its sister fair Frieze Masters, dedicated to work made before 2000. Also running throughout Regent’s Park at this time is Friez

name: Art Basel Paris
text: Art Basel ParisImage Credit: Telmo Pinto/SOPA Images/LightRocket via Getty ImagesSince launching in 2022, Art Basel Paris (née Paris+ par Art Basel) has slowly begun to eclipse its older sibling, Art Basel’s original Swiss edition, with many American collectors preferring the French capital to Basel. Now firmly established in its permanent home, the iconic Grand Palais, the 2026 edition will be do

name: Artissima, Turin
text: Artissima, TurinImage Credit: Courtesy ArtissimaEstablished in 1994, Artissima is one of Italy’s most important art fairs. The fair is spilt into a number of sections, including its Main Section, Monologue/Dialogue, and New Entries, as well as curated sections chosen by a panel of curators.Location: OVAL Lingotto FierePreview Day: Thursday, October 29General Admission Days: Friday, October 30–Sund

name: Art Cologne
text: Art CologneImage Credit: ©Koelnmesse GmbH, Thomas KlerxThe world’s oldest art fair celebrates its 59th edition in 2026. The 2025 edition of the fair featured 167 galleries from 25 countries, and welcomed some 45,000 visitors during its run. “The art market is stable and presents itself as fit for the future. Cologne is the best proof of this,” Art Cologne director Daniel Hug said in a statement af

name: Art Collaboration Kyoto
text: Art Collaboration KyotoImage Credit: Moriya Yuki/Courtesy ACKSince its launch in 2021, Art Collaboration Kyoto’s star has been on the rise. Many observers have called it one of the world’s best art fairs, citing the charm of Kyoto and the emphasis on collaboration and a rethinking of the art fair model.Location: Kyoto International Conference CenterVIP dates: Friday, November 6General Admission Da

name: ADAA Fair, New York
text: ADAA Fair, New YorkImage Credit: Jason WycheIn July 2025, the Art Dealers Association of America made the startling decision to cancel its annual art fair, called the Art Show. That cancelation was termed a “strategic pause” by its board, likely having to do, in part, with the ADAA ending its partnership with Henry Street Settlement. (For years, the Art Show’s opening night severed as a benefit fo

name: Frieze Abu Dhabi
text: Frieze Abu DhabiImage Credit: ©Department of Culture and Tourism, Abu DhabiIn October 2025, Friezeannouncedthat it would take over the homegrown Abu Dhabi Art fair, working in collaboration with the Department of Culture and Tourism – Abu Dhabi (DCT Abu Dhabi). Because that fair launched in 2007, Frieze is coming into the region with a certain amount of groundwork already laid. (Its competitor, Ar

name: Miami Art Week
text: Miami Art WeekImage Credit: Maximilíano Durón/ARTnewsTo close out each year, the art world—and industries like fashion and entertainment—descend on Miami Beach in early December for a bevy of art fairs. The most important of these is Art Basel Miami Beach, which has been led by former dealer Bridget Finn since 2023. The other two notable satellite fairs aligned with ABMB are Untitled Art, which se
